result.markdown:
--- title: "Causal inference
with conjoints" format: revealjs: theme: [simple, ../../css/slide-styles.scss, brand] width: 1600 height: 900 auto-stretch: false preview-links: true code-block-height: 730px controls: auto title-slide-attributes: data-background-image: ../../img/background-hex-shapes.svg data-background-opacity: "0.5" data-background-gradient: "linear-gradient(30deg, {{< brand color orange >}}, {{< brand color yellow >}})" include-in-header: - text: | --- # Sliders and switches {background-color='{{< brand color yellow >}}' background-image='../../img/background-hex-shapes.svg' background-opacity='0.5'} ## \ ![](img/slider-switch-annotated-80.jpg){fig-align="center" width="100%" style="box-shadow: 5px 5px 15px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.3); border-radius: 5px;"} ## \ ![](img/mixer-board-annotated-80.jpg){fig-align="center" width="100%" style="box-shadow: 5px 5px 15px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.3); border-radius: 5px;"} ## ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(tidyverse) library(marginaleffects) library(parameters) penguins <- penguins |> drop_na(sex) model1 <- lm(body_mass ~ flipper_len, data = penguins) model2 <- lm(body_mass ~ flipper_len + species, data = penguins) ``` ::: . . . \ ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} model_parameters(model1) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Parameter | Coefficient | SE | 95% CI | t(331) | p --------------------------------------------------------------------------- (Intercept) | -5872.09 | 310.29 | [-6482.47, -5261.71] | -18.92 | < .001 flipper len | 50.15 | 1.54 | [ 47.12, 53.18] | 32.56 | < .001 ``` ::: ::: . . . \ ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} model_parameters(model2) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Parameter | Coefficient | SE | 95% CI | t(329) | p ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- (Intercept) | -4013.18 | 586.25 | [-5166.44, -2859.92] | -6.85 | < .001 flipper len | 40.61 | 3.08 | [ 34.55, 46.66] | 13.19 | < .001 species [Chinstrap] | -205.38 | 57.57 | [ -318.62, -92.13] | -3.57 | < .001 species [Gentoo] | 284.52 | 95.43 | [ 96.79, 472.25] | 2.98 | 0.003 ``` ::: ::: ## {marginaleffects} ::: {.box} Quit working with raw coefficients! ::: ::: {.text-tiny} Arel-Bundock, Vincent, Noah Greifer, and Andrew Heiss. 2024. “How to Interpret Statistical Models Using marginaleffects for R and Python.” Journal of Statistical Software 111 (9): 1–32. . Arel-Bundock, Vincent. 2026. Model to Meaning: How to Interpret Statistical Models with R and Python. A Chapman & Hall Book. CRC Press, Taylor & Francis Group. , ::: ![](img/flow-mer@3x.png){fig-align="center" width="100%" style="box-shadow: 5px 5px 15px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.3); border-radius: 5px;"} ## 1. **Quantity**: What is the quantity of interest? Do we want to report a prediction or a function of predictions (average, difference, ratio, derivative, etc.)? 2. **Grid**: What predictor values are we interested in? Do we want to report estimates for the units in our dataset, or for hypothetical or representative individuals? 3. **Aggregation**: Do we report estimates for every observation in the grid or a global summary? 4. **Uncertainty**: How do we quantify uncertainty about our estimates? 5. **Test**: Which (non-)linear hypothesis or equivalence tests do we conduct? ## ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} model_parameters(model1) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Parameter | Coefficient | SE | 95% CI | t(331) | p --------------------------------------------------------------------------- (Intercept) | -5872.09 | 310.29 | [-6482.47, -5261.71] | -18.92 | < .001 flipper len | 50.15 | 1.54 | [ 47.12, 53.18] | 32.56 | < .001 ``` ::: ::: . . . \ ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} avg_comparisons(model1) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Estimate Std. Error z Pr(>|z|) S 2.5 % 97.5 % 50.2 1.54 32.6 <0.001 770.2 47.1 53.2 Term: flipper_len Type: response Comparison: +1 ``` ::: ::: ## ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} model_parameters(model2) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Parameter | Coefficient | SE | 95% CI | t(329) | p ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- (Intercept) | -4013.18 | 586.25 | [-5166.44, -2859.92] | -6.85 | < .001 flipper len | 40.61 | 3.08 | [ 34.55, 46.66] | 13.19 | < .001 species [Chinstrap] | -205.38 | 57.57 | [ -318.62, -92.13] | -3.57 | < .001 species [Gentoo] | 284.52 | 95.43 | [ 96.79, 472.25] | 2.98 | 0.003 ``` ::: ::: . . . \ ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} avg_comparisons(model2) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Term Contrast Estimate Std. Error z Pr(>|z|) S 2.5 % 97.5 % flipper_len +1 40.6 3.08 13.19 < 0.001 129.5 34.6 46.6 species Chinstrap - Adelie -205.4 57.57 -3.57 < 0.001 11.4 -318.2 -92.5 species Gentoo - Adelie 284.5 95.43 2.98 0.00287 8.4 97.5 471.6 Type: response ``` ::: ::: ## ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} model3 <-lm(body_mass ~ flipper_len * species, data = penguins) model_parameters(model3) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Parameter | Coefficient | SE | 95% CI | t(327) | p -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- (Intercept) | -2508.09 | 892.41 | [-4263.68, -752.49] | -2.81 | 0.005 flipper len | 32.69 | 4.69 | [ 23.46, 41.92] | 6.97 | < .001 species [Chinstrap] | -529.11 | 1525.11 | [-3529.37, 2471.16] | -0.35 | 0.729 species [Gentoo] | -4166.12 | 1431.58 | [-6982.39, -1349.84] | -2.91 | 0.004 flipper len × species [Chinstrap] | 1.88 | 7.86 | [ -13.59, 17.36] | 0.24 | 0.811 flipper len × species [Gentoo] | 21.48 | 6.97 | [ 7.77, 35.18] | 3.08 | 0.002 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} avg_comparisons(model3, by = "species") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Term Contrast species Estimate Std. Error z Pr(>|z|) S 2.5 % 97.5 % flipper_len +1 Adelie 32.7 4.69 6.967 < 0.001 38.2 23.5 41.9 flipper_len +1 Chinstrap 34.6 6.31 5.478 < 0.001 24.5 22.2 46.9 flipper_len +1 Gentoo 54.2 5.15 10.516 < 0.001 83.5 44.1 64.3 species Chinstrap - Adelie Adelie -170.9 65.04 -2.627 0.00861 6.9 -298.3 -43.4 species Gentoo - Adelie Adelie -83.4 146.97 -0.567 0.57052 0.8 -371.4 204.7 species Chinstrap - Adelie Chinstrap -160.1 60.39 -2.651 0.00803 7.0 -278.4 -41.7 species Gentoo - Adelie Chinstrap 39.5 122.28 0.323 0.74675 0.4 -200.2 279.2 species Chinstrap - Adelie Gentoo -119.7 193.37 -0.619 0.53580 0.9 -498.7 259.3 species Gentoo - Adelie Gentoo 499.3 135.18 3.694 < 0.001 12.1 234.4 764.3 Type: response ``` ::: ::: ## ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} plot_predictions(model3, condition = c("flipper_len", "species")) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](slides_files/figure-revealjs/unnamed-chunk-10-1.png){width=100%} ::: ::: # What are marginal means? {background-color='{{< brand color yellow >}}' background-image='../../img/background-hex-shapes.svg' background-opacity='0.5'} ## OLS with categorical predictors ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} model <- lm(body_mass ~ species + sex, data = penguins) ``` ::: ## Predictions across balanced grid ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} grid <- datagrid(model = model, species = unique, sex = unique) preds <- predictions(model, grid) ``` ::: \ :::: {.columns} ::: {.column .fragment} ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} preds |> as_tibble() |> select(species, sex, estimate) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` # A tibble: 6 × 3 species sex estimate 1 Adelie female 3372. 2 Adelie male 4040. 3 Chinstrap female 3399. 4 Chinstrap male 4067. 5 Gentoo female 4750. 6 Gentoo male 5418. ``` ::: ::: ::: ::: {.column .fragment} ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
Sex
Species Female Male
Adelie 3372 4040
Chinstrap 3399 4067
Gentoo 4750 5418
``` ::: ::: ::: :::: ## Means in the margins ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
Sex
Species Female Male Marginal mean
Adelie 3372 4040 3706
(3372 + 4040) / 2
Chinstrap 3399 4067 3733
(3399 + 4067) / 2
Gentoo 4750 5418 5084
(4750 + 5418) / 2
Marginal mean 3841
(3372 + 3399 + 4750) / 3		 4508
(4040 + 4067 + 5418) / 3
``` ::: ::: ::: {.box style="font-size: 0.7em;"} For the sex-specific averages, all the between-species variation is "marginalized out" or accounted for; for the species-specific averages, all the between-sex variation is similarly marginalized out. ::: ## Differences in marginal means ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
Sex
Species Female Male Marginal mean ∆
Adelie 3372 4040 3706
(3372 + 4040) / 2		 —
Chinstrap 3399 4067 3733
(3399 + 4067) / 2		 27
Chinstrap−Adelie
Gentoo 4750 5418 5084
(4750 + 5418) / 2		 1378
Gentoo−Adelie
Marginal mean 3841
(3372 + 3399 + 4750) / 3		 4508
(4040 + 4067 + 5418) / 3
∆ — 668
Male−Female
``` ::: ::: ## Coefficients and predictions ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} parameters::model_parameters(model) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Parameter | Coefficient | SE | 95% CI | t(329) | p -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- (Intercept) | 3372.39 | 31.43 | [3310.56, 3434.21] | 107.31 | < .001 species [Chinstrap] | 26.92 | 46.48 | [ -64.52, 118.37] | 0.58 | 0.563 species [Gentoo] | 1377.86 | 39.10 | [1300.93, 1454.78] | 35.24 | < .001 sex [male] | 667.56 | 34.70 | [ 599.29, 735.82] | 19.24 | < .001 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} avg_predictions(model, by = "species", newdata = "balanced") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` species Estimate Std. Error z Pr(>|z|) S 2.5 % 97.5 % Adelie 3706 26.2 141.4 <0.001 Inf 3655 3758 Chinstrap 3733 38.4 97.2 <0.001 Inf 3658 3808 Gentoo 5084 29.0 175.1 <0.001 Inf 5027 5141 Type: response ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} avg_predictions(model, by = "sex", newdata = "balanced") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` sex Estimate Std. Error z Pr(>|z|) S 2.5 % 97.5 % female 3841 25.3 152 <0.001 Inf 3791 3890 male 4508 25.1 180 <0.001 Inf 4459 4557 Type: response ``` ::: :::

include-after-body:
<script>
  // htmlwidgets need to know to resize themselves when slides are shown/hidden.
  // Fire the "slideenter" event (handled by htmlwidgets.js) when the current
  // slide changes (different for each slide format).
  (function () {
    // dispatch for htmlwidgets
    function fireSlideEnter() {
      const event = window.document.createEvent("Event");
      event.initEvent("slideenter", true, true);
      window.document.dispatchEvent(event);
    }

    function fireSlideChanged(previousSlide, currentSlide) {
      fireSlideEnter();

      // dispatch for shiny
      if (window.jQuery) {
        if (previousSlide) {
          window.jQuery(previousSlide).trigger("hidden");
        }
        if (currentSlide) {
          window.jQuery(currentSlide).trigger("shown");
        }
      }
    }

    // hookup for slidy
    if (window.w3c_slidy) {
      window.w3c_slidy.add_observer(function (slide_num) {
        // slide_num starts at position 1
        fireSlideChanged(null, w3c_slidy.slides[slide_num - 1]);
      });
    }

  })();
</script>
